category_title: Elementary-aged children with mild/moderate autism and significant language delays
minimal_context: The student and a peer are playing with grocery store toys on the carpet in a sectioned-off hallway.
dialogue:
TUTOR: We are going to play with the grocery store toys on the carpet for ten minutes. You and your partner can play together.

STUDENT: (Picks up a plastic apple) Red apple.

TUTOR: Your partner has the shopping basket.

STUDENT: (Looks at the basket) Basket.

TUTOR: What can you say to your partner?

STUDENT: (Reaches for the basket without asking) Put apple.

options.A: Ask the children specific questions about how they are playing with the grocery toys and what verbal exchanges have occurred, such as prompting the student to ask their partner for the basket.
options.B: Help the student compliment their partner and rephrase any hurtful comments into something more friendly during the ten-minute play session.
options.C: Work on the student's recognition of body language and facial cues while, at the same time, labeling feelings during the ten-minute play session.
options.D: Have more new, motivating grocery store toys handy to introduce to the children during the ten-minute play session.
